examples.title: TLS Handshake Protocol (Simplified)
examples.description: Pseudocode representing the TLS 1.2 full handshake process
examples.code:
PROCEDURE TLSHandshake()
BEGIN
    // Phase 1: Hello
    clientRandom ← GenerateRandom(32)
    clientHello ← CreateClientHello(clientRandom, supportedCipherSuites)
    Send(clientHello)
    
    serverHello ← Receive()
    serverRandom ← serverHello.random
    selectedCipher ← serverHello.cipherSuite
    
    // Phase 2: Certificate Exchange
    serverCert ← Receive()
    VerifyCertificate(serverCert)
    serverPublicKey ← ExtractPublicKey(serverCert)
    
    IF selectedCipher requires key exchange THEN
        serverKeyParams ← Receive()
    END IF
    
    serverHelloDone ← Receive()
    
    // Phase 3: Key Exchange
    premasterSecret ← GenerateRandom(48)
    encryptedPremaster ← Encrypt(premasterSecret, serverPublicKey)
    Send(ClientKeyExchange, encryptedPremaster)
    
    // Phase 4: Master Secret Derivation
    masterSecret ← PRF(premasterSecret, "master secret", 
                        clientRandom + serverRandom)
    
    // Phase 5: Session Keys Derivation
    keyBlock ← PRF(masterSecret, "key expansion", 
                   clientRandom + serverRandom)
    clientWriteMACKey ← Extract(keyBlock, 0, hashLength)
    serverWriteMACKey ← Extract(keyBlock, hashLength, hashLength)
    clientWriteKey ← Extract(keyBlock, 2*hashLength, keyLength)
    serverWriteKey ← Extract(keyBlock, 2*hashLength+keyLength, keyLength)
    
    // Phase 6: Change Cipher Spec & Finished
    Send(ChangeCipherSpec)
    
    handshakeHash ← Hash(all handshake messages)
    finished ← PRF(masterSecret, "client finished", handshakeHash)
    encryptedFinished ← Encrypt(finished, clientWriteKey, clientWriteMACKey)
    Send(encryptedFinished)
    
    // Server side
    Receive(ChangeCipherSpec)
    Receive(encryptedFinished)
    VerifyFinished(encryptedFinished, serverWriteKey, serverWriteMACKey)
    
    Send(ChangeCipherSpec)
    encryptedFinishedServer ← Encrypt(finished, serverWriteKey, serverWriteMACKey)
    Send(encryptedFinishedServer)
    
    RETURN established session with masterSecret and session keys
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the full TLS handshake including random generation, certificate exchange, key derivation using PRF, and the ChangeCipherSpec transition. The master secret is derived from the premaster secret using the PRF function with both random values as seeds.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per handshake (constant number of operations)

examples.title: Python SSL/TLS Client Implementation
examples.description: Using Python's ssl module to establish secure HTTPS connection
examples.code:
import ssl
import socket

# Create SSL context with certificate verification
context = ssl.SSLContext(ssl.PROTOCOL_TLS_CLIENT)
context.load_verify_locations('/etc/ssl/certs/ca-certificates.crt')

# Create socket and wrap with SSL
with socket.create_connection(('www.example.com', 443)) as sock:
    with context.wrap_socket(sock, server_hostname='www.example.com') as ssock:
        # Display connection details
        print(f"Protocol: {ssock.version()}")
        print(f"Cipher: {ssock.cipher()}")
        print(f"Certificate: {ssock.getpeercert()}")
        
        # Send HTTPS request
        request = "GET / HTTP/1.1\r\nHost: www.example.com\r\nConnection: close\r\n\r\n"
        ssock.sendall(request.encode())
        
        # Receive response
        response = b""
        while True:
            data = ssock.recv(4096)
            if not data:
                break
            response += data
        
        print(response.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore'))
examples.explanation: This Python code demonstrates establishing a secure TLS connection using the ssl module. It creates an SSL context with certificate verification, connects to an HTTPS server, retrieves connection details including cipher suite and certificate information, and performs an HTTP request over TLS.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for data transmission, O(1) for connection setup

examples.title: C Language SSL/TLS Server (OpenSSL)
examples.description: Simple SSL/TLS server implementation using OpenSSL library
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates a simple TLS server using OpenSSL. It initializes SSL, creates a context with TLS 1.2 minimum version and secure cipher suites, binds to a port, accepts connections, performs TLS handshake, and exchanges data securely. Key functions include SSL_new(), SSL_accept(), SSL_read(), and SSL_write().
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for data transfer, O(1) for handshake
